title: Vaccine Storage and Handling Resources | Vaccines & Immunizations | CDC
text:
Aug. 10, 2024 
At a glance 
Proper vaccine storage and handling practices play a very important role in protecting individuals and communities from vaccine-preventable diseases. Vaccine quality is the shared responsibility of everyone, from the time vaccine is manufactured until it is administered.
Resources 
Storage Labels 
Organizing vaccines within the storage unit with labels is a best practice that can minimize vaccination errors. These example vaccine labels can help staff quickly identify vaccines and beyond-use date information, if applicable. 
Vaccine Labels (June 2025)
2024-2025 Seasonal Respiratory Illnesses Vaccine Labels (June 2025)
Vaccine Storage Temperatures 
This new one-pager includes the recommended temperatures for vaccines stored in the refrigerator, freezer, and ultra-cold freezer. This resource includes the vaccine and product trade names for easier identification. 
Recommended Vaccine Storage Temperatures 
Storage Best Practices 
These storage and handling fact sheets illustrate best practices for refrigerated and frozen vaccines. Written in plain language, they include assessments to reinforce key points. While they are CDC-developed and branded fact sheets, each contains an area where you can insert your agency's logo.
Refrigerated Vaccines Storage Best Practices
Fahrenheit (F) | Celsius (C) 
Frozen Vaccines Storage Best Practices 
Fahrenheit (F) | Cel
